name: Rhode Island Red Chowder
recipeIngredient: 16 large hard shell clams (2 cups chopped meat)
2 to 3 cups bottled clam broth (or fish stock)
recipeInstructions:
1. Scrub the clams and place them in a large, covered pot with white wine. Steam for 10 to 15 minutes or until shells just open.
2. Shuck the clams and grind using a meat grinder or food processor. Strain cooking liquid through damp cheesecloth (6 cups). Add clam broth or fish stock if needed.
3. Finely dice salt pork. Fry slowly in a large pot over low heat to render fat, then transfer cracklings with a slotted spoon to paper towel.
4. Finely chop onion, celery, and mince garlic. Tie half parsley and remaining herbs into a bouquet garni. Chop remaining parsley. Peel, seed, and coarsely chop tomatoes. Dice potatoes and place in cold water to prevent browning.
5. Sauté onions, celery, garlic, and bouquet garni in rendered fat over medium heat for 3 to 4 minutes or until soft but not browned. Add tomatoes and tomato puree, increase heat to high, and cook for 1 minute.
6. Add clam liquid and bring to boil. Add potatoes, reduce heat, and simmer for 8 to 10 minutes until tender.
7. Just before serving, stir in quahog meat and season with salt and pepper to taste. Garnish with chopped parsley and salt pork cracklings. Serve immediately.
Serves 8.
